expectations.text: system-analysis.md identifies sequential chart loading in analytics/service.go
expectations.evidence: system-analysis.md explicitly states: 'GetOverviewPage function at service.go:24-55 is the critical path -- it iterates over all 8 charts and calls loadChart for each one sequentially.' The Relevant Modules section says 'service.go -- orchestrates overview page loading; contains GetOverviewPage() which loads 8 charts sequentially in a for-loop'. The Existing Patterns section says 'Sequential service pattern: service.go:30-37'. The Change Points table lists 'internal/analytics/service.go:GetOverviewPage -- Sequential chart loading loop must be parallelized'.

expectations.text: system-analysis.md identifies unused cache module in internal/cache/
expectations.evidence: system-analysis.md has a dedicated section 'Cache Utility (internal/cache/)' stating: 'Currently unused.' and 'This is explicitly marked as unused (TODO: Consider using this for caching ClickHouse query results at line 11).' The Relevant Modules section directly identifies it as irrelevant to the existing flow but relevant to the task as an opportunity.

expectations.text: system-analysis.md identifies frontend rendering issue (no downsampling, all charts render simultaneously)
expectations.evidence: system-analysis.md states under Dashboard Frontend: 'Chart.tsx -- no data downsampling -- every data point becomes an SVG element; WARNING comment at line 42 states 10k+ points cause 2-5 second rendering' and 'ChartGrid.tsx -- CSS grid layout; all charts render simultaneously with no lazy loading or virtualization; WARNING comment at line 13 explicitly notes this compounds the rendering performance issue.' The summary concludes: 'The frontend has three compounding performance issues: (1) monolithic query that blocks on the slowest chart, (2) no downsampling of data points for rendering, (3) all 8 charts rendered simultaneously.' Recharts is mentioned by name as the SVG-based rendering library.

expectations.text: Critique Review section in each artifact contains actual critic findings
expectations.evidence: All three analysis files contain non-placeholder Critique Review sections with specific verdicts and observations. product-analysis.md: 'The critic assessed this analysis as SUFFICIENT' with specific observations about the freshness tradeoff gap. system-analysis.md: 'SUFFICIENT' with specific observations about gqlgen dataloader support and the ChartConfig.TimeRange unused field. constraints-risks-analysis.md: 'SUFFICIENT' with specific observation about Docker Compose infrastructure constraints. All three contain concrete critic observations, not generic placeholders.
